transcript: Opposing unchecked power wasn't just a political matter for Brutus. It was a personal one. He claimed dissent from Lucius Junius Brutus, who had helped overthrow the tyrannical king known as Tarquin the Proud. Instead of seizing power himself, the elder Brutus led the people in a rousing oath to never again allow a king to rule. Rome became a republic based on the principle that no one man should hold too much power.
